question: When translating a BRD item about 'sending reminders when renewal premium is insufficient', what technical details must the TRD define?
What is the 'Contract First' principle in API design?
What are the three quality gates for BRD submission?
What is the naming convention for Java constants? Give an example.
What are the three pillars of observability in the logging standard?
How many DDL change risk levels are there? Who approves L3 changes?
What are the recommended SLA targets for core system API response times?
What is the recommended feature branch lifetime in Trunk-Based Development?
What are the response time and escalation requirements for a P0 (disaster) incident?
What immediate actions must be taken for financial loss incidents?
What must be verified in the rollback plan before release?
What are the three types of performance tests and their recommended cadences?
What six design principles should test cases follow?
ground_truths: Trigger timing (T+N days), notification channels (SMS/App push/email), notification frequency (daily/one-time), and failure retry strategy.
Define interface documentation before implementing the code, ensuring both parties share the same understanding before development begins.
All business rules must cover normal and exception flows; accounting items must include journal entry examples; regulatory reporting items must include sample regulatory messages.
UPPER_SNAKE_CASE, all uppercase letters separated by underscores. Example: MAX_RETRY_COUNT.
Logging (discrete event records), Metrics (aggregatable numerical indicators), and Tracing (request-level call chain tracking).
Three levels: L1 low, L2 medium, L3 high. L3 changes (dropping tables/columns, modifying primary keys, large table changes) require Architecture Review Board approval.
P50 < 500ms, P99 < 2000ms, error rate < 0.1% for core interfaces.
Feature branches should have a short lifetime, no more than 3 days.
P0 requires immediate response (0 minutes) and escalation within 10 minutes.
Immediate stop-loss (halt transactions/degrade functionality), notify finance department, preserve all logs and database snapshots, involve legal for loss and compliance assessment.
Application rollback steps (to previous version), database rollback scripts, data rollback trigger criteria, estimated RTO, and post-rollback data validity.
Regular load tests before major releases, peak stress tests 1 month before high season, and capacity assessments quarterly.
Equivalence partitioning, boundary value analysis, decision tables, state transition, exception scenarios, and data-driven testing.
